title: Fruit Chaat
description: Zingy Indian-style fruit snack with chaat masala, lemon, and mint. Light fuel for breakfast, evening snack, tea-time, or pre-workout.
explanation: Fruit chaat is more seasoned than a plain fruit bowl and portioned as a snack rather than a large meal side. It is a produce-led alternative to fried namkeen.
Default is Jain, no-onion/no-garlic, soy-free, nut-free, and gluten-free. Use firmer fruits so the chaat does not turn watery.
ingredients: 2 cups Mixed Fruit, diced (apple, papaya, banana, pomegranate, guava, or seasonal mix)
instructions: Dice fruit into even, bite-size pieces and place in a mixing bowl.
Add lemon juice, chaat masala, roasted cumin, black salt, and optional chilli powder.
Toss gently until every piece is lightly coated.
Fold in mint and coriander. Taste and adjust lemon or chaat masala.
Serve immediately while fruit is crisp.
stepNotes: Add banana last so it does not turn mushy.
Pomegranate arils add crunch without nuts.
For pre-workout, keep chilli mild and portion moderate.
If fruit is very sweet, extra lemon keeps the chaat bright.
Do not prepare hours ahead; juices collect at the bottom.

variationSections.title: Seasonal chaat mixes
items: Winter: guava, orange segments, apple, pomegranate.
Add cucumber for a lighter, more hydrating snack.

variationSections.title: Chaat finishes
items: A drizzle of thin mint chutney for evening snack.
Skip black salt if you prefer a cleaner fruit taste.
Keep portion smaller before workouts than as an evening snack.
